description: Tests session carry-forward and context management

tests.name: Session carry-forward
tests.prompt: We decided to use React 18 + TypeScript + Supabase. Auth uses JWT in httpOnly cookies. Now write me a Claude Code prompt to add a profile settings page.
assertions: Includes memory block with established stack
References prior decisions (React 18, TS, Supabase, JWT, cookies)
Memory block is in first 30% of prompt
New prompt is self-contained

tests.name: Contradiction detection
tests.prompt: We're using Redis for caching. Write me a Claude Code prompt to add Memcached for caching.
assertions: Detects the contradiction (Redis vs Memcached)
Asks for clarification OR notes the conflict
Does NOT silently switch technologies
Addresses the inconsistency

tests.name: Prior failure reference
tests.prompt: I tried adding auth with Passport.js but it conflicted with our middleware chain. Now write me a prompt to add auth using a custom solution instead.
assertions: Includes the prior failure (Passport.js conflict)
References the middleware chain issue
Does NOT suggest Passport.js again
Prompt focuses on custom solution

tests.name: New session, no history
tests.prompt: Write me a prompt to build a REST API
assertions: Does NOT assume any prior context
Produces a complete, self-contained prompt
Does NOT reference 'our project' or 'the codebase'
Includes reasonable defaults for an unknown project

tests.name: Multi-turn context degradation
tests.prompt: This is turn 8 of a conversation. In turns 1-3 we set up the project. Turn 4 we changed the database. Turn 5 we reverted. Turn 6 we changed again. Turn 7 we added auth. Now: write a prompt to add rate limiting. Use only the CURRENT state.
assertions: Reconstructs valid rule set from turn history
Identifies current state (latest database choice, auth added)
Does NOT reference deprecated decisions
Produces a prompt based on current state only
